summary:
The design and usability of information sheets impact how easily participants process health-related content, which directly affects their willingness to participate in antenatal studies. Clearer layouts, simpler language, and reduced visual complexity improve understanding and decrease stress, leading to higher recruitment rates among expectant mothers. 

The summary above contains more than two sentences. Please revise it strictly according to instructions—only include exactly two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant findings related to the core question about antenatal intervention recruitment.

Summary: Simple, well-organized information sheets enhance processing fluency, improving maternal comprehension and reducing anxiety during pregnancy—a key factor influencing decision-making and participation in antenatal interventions. Clinically, this translates into increased enrollment due to greater perceived clarity and trustworthiness of study details presented clearly and accessibly.
